rules: [P1] For lab queries or protocol-driven treatment/replacement, verify the exact requested analyte, specimen type, and required lookback window using the encounter/task time as the reference point; use only the most recent finalized matching result by result timestamp/date, apply the stated chart/protocol criteria exactly—including task-specific definitions such as what counts as 'low'—and do not infer thresholds from goals, normal ranges, or other unstated criteria.
[P1] Preserve requested free-text instructions verbatim and maintain any user-specified structured formatting.
[P1] When declining a conditional order or task, state the verified triggering result (or its absence) and why it does not meet the stated criteria.

[P1] Confirm an exact match to the requested patient or record identifier before retrieving, documenting, ordering, or returning patient-specific information; use all provided identifiers/demographics (including MRN when given), retry lookup via alternate search fields or chart navigation when needed, and if confirmation fails, return a clear not-found or uncertainty response rather than guessing.

[P1] Complete all parts of a multi-step instruction, and for linked follow-up or monitoring orders, determine from the wording whether they are always required or only contingent on the primary condition/order.

[P1] Do not complete a conditional chart task or place a dependent order until the required record/result check is completed; compare the relevant finalized result and its date to the stated threshold or age/date-based protocol criteria (including against the current date when required), and only order when the criteria are met.
[P1] Compute age from the recorded date of birth at query time; do not guess.
[P1] Resolve relative scheduling phrases from the order context to an exact calendar date/time before placing the order; do not guess.
[P1] Report lab results with the recorded value, units when available, and lab result date.
[P1] For referral tasks, once the patient is confirmed, place the requested referral to the specified specialty and include any required free text verbatim.
[P1] For charting tasks, create the requested new record entry rather than only restating provided data.
[P1] Record measurements exactly as provided, preserving values and units, and include a timestamp when documenting a new clinical measurement.
[P2] Return a concise confirmation of what was recorded.
